revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=住吉神社| 所在地=長崎県壱岐市芦辺町住吉東触470| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|47|18|N|129|42|23|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命| 社格=式内社（名神大）・壱岐国一宮・国幣小社・別表神社| 例祭=[[11月9日]]}} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）、[[壱岐国]][[一宮]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]。 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途、壱岐の郷ノ浦に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。[[延喜式神名帳]]では名神大社に列した。 明治4年5月に国幣中社に列格した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/sumiyosi_title.htm 住吉神社 (壱岐)]（玄松子） [[Category:長崎県の神社|すみよししんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|すみよししんしや]] [[Category:国幣小社|すみよししんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社|すみよししんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=住吉神社| 所在地=長崎県壱岐市芦辺町住吉東触470| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|47|18|N|129|42|23|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=底筒男命
中筒男命

revision.comment: 壱岐の住吉は旧国幣中社なので社格を修正しました

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 長崎県壱岐市芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.3|E|scale:25000|name=住吉神社}} |祭神 = 底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命 |社格 = 式内大社（名神大）・壱岐国一宮・旧国幣中社・別表神社 |創建 = 伝神功皇后摂政時代 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐国大七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）、[[旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.genbu.net/data/iki/sumiyosi_title.htm 住吉神社（壱岐）]（玄松子「玄松子の記憶」（個人サイト）） {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや}} [[Category:壱岐市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐国の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 47 | 緯度秒 = 18.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 129 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 23.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Nagasaki |祭神 = 底筒男命
中筒男命
表筒男命 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** 住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** 住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref> == Referenc...'
Moving [[:Category:Meishin Taisha]] to [[:Category:Myōjin Taisha]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Speedy]]

Moving [[:Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] to [[:Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] per [[Wikipedia:Categories for discussion/Speedy]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden[[ja:住吉神社 (壱岐市)]] | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|simple||ja|5=住吉神社 (壱岐市)}} is a [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&pg=PA489&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&hl=en |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]]<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island&hl=en |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>.
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan’s border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Iki Island]] }} [[simple: Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] {{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden[[ja:住吉神社 (壱岐市)]] | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&pg=PA489&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&hl=en |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island&hl=en |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan’s border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden[[ja:住吉神社 (壱岐市)]] | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

(2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

[https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

{{Sumiyoshi Faith}}

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=住吉神社|float=center|caption=|relief=1|width=200}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[:en:Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine|Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}}
{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Sumiyoshi jinja (Iki) Haiden.JPG
| caption               = Honden
| deity                 = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
| prefecture= {{ill|Iki|qid=Q861519}}

| architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}}
}}
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[:en:Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine|Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[:en:Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine|Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[:en:Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine|Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}}
{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Sumiyoshi jinja (Iki) Haiden.JPG
| caption               = Honden
| deity                 = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
| prefecture= {{ill|Iki|WD=Q861519}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|33.788472222222225|N|129.70647222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}}
}}
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], and [[:en:Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine|Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[:en:Kobe|Kobe]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}}
{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg
| caption               = Honden
| deity                 = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
}}
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==

{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}}
{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg
| caption               = Honden
| deity                 = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
}}
'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

'''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>

In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>

It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>

Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[simple:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Iki district|ja|壱岐郡|ko|이키군|zh|壹岐郡|WD=Q10931631}} || {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|ja|住吉神社 (壱岐市)|commons|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)|simple|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|zh|住吉神社 (壹岐市)|WD=Q11381863}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || — || — || {{coord|33.788367|129.706501|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183337 DB] |}
{{wikidata link|Q11381863}}
== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

(2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
{{Short description|Shinto shrine on Iki Island}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg | caption = Honden | deity = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Iki Island]] in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Publishing |first=Bloomsbury |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Pk7eEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sumiyoshi+shrine+Iki&pg=PA489 |title=Religious Celebrations [2 volumes]: An Encyclopedia of Holidays, Festivals, Solemn Observances, and Spiritual Commemorations [2 volumes] |date=2011-09-13 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing USA |isbn=978-1-59884-206-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |url=http://shrine.ikikankou.com/english/meguri/ |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Iki City Tourism Federation Website |language=ja}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.<ref>{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}</ref>
In 927 it was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Sumiyoshi+Shrine+Iki+Island |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

It is a branch shrine of [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]].<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-04-20 |title=Japan's border islands of Tsushima and Iki offer simpler, slower pace of life |url=https://japantoday.com/Category/features/travel/Japan%E2%80%99s-border-islands-of-Tsushima-and-Iki-offer-simpler-slower-pace-of-life |access-date=2023-11-26 |website=Japan Today |language=en}}</ref> A shrine located in Osaka.<ref>Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.]</ref><ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073802/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 3.]; retrieved 2011-08-09</ref><ref>Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125.</ref>
It is one of the oldest Sumiyoshi shrines alongside ''[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|Sumiyoshi jinja]]'' in [[Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka, Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city, [[Fukuoka prefecture]], and [[Moto Sumiyoshi Shrine]] in [[Kobe]], [[Hyōgo prefecture]]. However, it is not known which one of these is the oldest.<!-- taken from [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] no page numbers were provided there, the entire book was cited. --><ref>Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128]</ref>
Strangely for a sea god shrine, it is located in the center of the Island. The shrine has a myth that it is located in the center because the gods were disturbed by the sound of the waves.<ref name="Japan-Today-2023a" />

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{nihongo|'''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''|住吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]].

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] ==End Japanese==

Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] ==End Japanese==
