[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

[[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|住吉神社 (壱岐市)|version=107359698|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[底筒男命]]
[[中筒男命]]
[[表筒男命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[国幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = [[11月9日]] |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町住吉東触にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣中社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]」と総称する。[[大国主|八千戈神]]を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって[[三韓征伐]]を為し遂げた[[神功皇后]]が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、[[神託]]により現在地に遷座した。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = {{jalink|11月9日|Q2991}} |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|国幣中社}}で、現在は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}の{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}}。 == 祭神 == 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}}」と総称する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|八千戈神}}を相殿に祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって{{jalink|三韓征伐|Q11249435}}を為し遂げた{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}により現在地に遷座した。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 == 例祭 == * 例大祭 - 11月9日 == 文化財 == === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』{{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}}、1979年、190頁 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|底筒男命}}
{{jalink|中筒男命}}

旧{{jalink|国幣中社}}
{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = {{jalink|11月9日|Q2991}} |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|国幣中社}}で、現在は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}の{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}}。 === 祭神 === 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}}」と総称する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|八千戈神}}を相殿に祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって{{jalink|三韓征伐|Q11249435}}を為し遂げた{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}により現在地に遷座した。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 === 例祭 === * 例大祭 - 11月9日 === 文化財 === === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連図書 === * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』{{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}}、1979年、190頁 === 外部リンク === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|底筒男命}}
{{jalink|中筒男命}}
{{jalink|表筒男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|国幣中社}}
{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = {{jalink|11月9日|Q2991}} |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|国幣中社}}で、現在は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}の{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}}。 === 祭神 === 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}}」と総称する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|八千戈神}}を相殿に祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって{{jalink|三韓征伐|Q11249435}}を為し遂げた{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}により現在地に遷座した。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 === 例祭 === * 例大祭 - 11月9日 === 文化財 === === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連図書 === * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』{{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}}、1979年、190頁 === 外部リンク === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with simplewiki or enwiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|底筒男命}}

旧{{jalink|国幣中社}}
{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = {{jalink|11月9日|Q2991}} |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|国幣中社}}で、現在は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}の{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}}。 === 祭神 === 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}}」と総称する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|八千戈神}}を相殿に祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって{{jalink|三韓征伐|Q11249435}}を為し遂げた{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}により現在地に遷座した。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 === 例祭 === * 例大祭 - 11月9日 === 文化財 === === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連図書 === * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』{{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}}、1979年、190頁 === 外部リンク === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|底筒男命}}
{{jalink|中筒男命}}
{{jalink|表筒男命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|国幣中社}}
{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}摂政年間 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = 壱岐七社 |例祭 = {{jalink|11月9日|Q2991}} |神事 = 軍越（いくさごえ）神事（1月17日・5月28日・10月27日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=住吉神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|長崎県|Q169376}}{{jalink|壱岐市|Q861519}}芦辺町住吉東触にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|国幣中社}}で、現在は{{jalink|神社本庁|Q908077}}の{{jalink|別表神社|Q10898274}}。 === 祭神 === 底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命を主祭神とし、「{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉大神}}」と総称する。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|八千戈神}}を相殿に祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、住吉大神の守護によって{{jalink|三韓征伐|Q11249435}}を為し遂げた{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}が、その帰途現在の壱岐市郷ノ浦町大浦触に上陸して三神を祀ったのに始まるという（これを以て「日本初の住吉神社」を称している）。その後、{{jalink|神託|Q217123}}により現在地に遷座した。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では名神大社に列した。その他にも長崎県下筆頭神社を名乗っている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）5月に国幣中社に列格した。 === 例祭 === * 例大祭 - 11月9日 === 文化財 === === 長崎県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 絹本着色高野四社明神像（絵画） - 室町時代中期以降の作。明治27年（1894年）大阪在住の芦辺町出身者から寄進。昭和50年（1975年）3月4日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 20。。 === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 住吉神社の神鏡 17面（工芸品） - 明治4年（1871年）に住吉神社境内の神池から発見された鏡。内訳は大陸系鏡12面、和鏡系5面。昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定境内説明板。『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 90。。 ** 御幸船（工芸品） - 住吉神社例祭の舟祭神事で使用される船。昭和8年（1933年）の新造『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 98。。 * 天然記念物 ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|参道から見た拝殿]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|拝殿前の「夫婦楠」]]住吉神社のクスノキ 2本 - 昭和51年（1976年）1月1日指定『壱岐市の文化財』 長崎県壱岐市教育委員会、2017年、p. 196。。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連図書 === * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』{{jalink|東京堂出版|Q11524645}}、1979年、190頁 === 外部リンク === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html 住吉神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}
{{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{jalink|November 9|Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}, {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}, and it is now a {{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} of the {{jalink|Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}, who accomplished {{jalink|Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964|major myōjin shrine}})
former {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}
{{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{jalink|November 9|Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}, {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}, and it is now a {{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} of the {{jalink|Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}, who accomplished {{jalink|Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}{{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964|major myōjin shrine}})
former {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}
{{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{jalink|November 9|Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}, {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}, and it is now a {{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} of the {{jalink|Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}, who accomplished {{jalink|Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]]

Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}{{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964|major myōjin shrine}})
former {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}
{{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{jalink|November 9|Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{jalink|Iki|Q861519}}, {{jalink|Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|kokuhei chūsha}}, and it is now a {{jalink|Beppyō shrine|Q10898274}} of the {{jalink|Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}, who accomplished {{jalink|Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{jalink|divine oracle|Q217123}}. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{jalink|Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町住吉東触470', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} [[EN:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] [[JA:住吉神社 (壱岐市)]] [[SIMPLE:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや いきし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

'長崎県壱岐市芦辺町住吉東触470', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} [[EN:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] [[JA:住吉神社 (壱岐市)]] [[SIMPLE:Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] [[ZH:住吉神社 (壹岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:国幣中社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]
