{{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:壱岐島の式内社|名すみよししんしや]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:神功皇后]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]][[Category:qq]]

Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]]

{{wikidata link|Q11381863}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sumiyoshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki), haiden.jpg|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}Ashibe-chō Sumiyoshi Higashifure 470 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|18.5|N|129|42|23.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = (trad.) regency period of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = |札所等 = Iki Seven Shrines |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 9|Q2991|WD=Q2991|1=November 9}} |神事 = Ikusagoe ritual (January 17, May 28, October 27) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Sumiyoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (すみよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sumiyoshi Higashifure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === Sokotsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto, and Uwatsutsu-no-o-no-Mikoto are the main deities, collectively called the "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}." {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} is enshrined in the associated hall. === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, who accomplished {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} under the protection of the Sumiyoshi deities, landed on her return at present Ōura-fure, Gōnoura-chō, Iki City and enshrined the three deities there (hence the claim of being "Japan's first Sumiyoshi Shrine"). It later moved to the current location by {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' it was ranked as myōjin taisha. It also claims to be the leading shrine in Nagasaki Prefecture. In May {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked kokuhei chūsha. === Annual festival === * Grand annual festival - November 9 === Cultural properties === === Nagasaki Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tangible cultural property ** Silk painting of the Four Kōya Shrine Deities (painting) - made after the middle Muromachi period. Donated in Meiji 27 (1894) by a donor from Ashibe-chō living in Osaka. Designated March 4, Shōwa 50 (1975)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.20.. === Iki City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** 17 sacred mirrors of Sumiyoshi Shrine (crafts) - mirrors found in 1871 in the sacred pond within Sumiyoshi Shrine precincts. Breakdown: 12 continental-style mirrors, 5 Japanese-style mirrors. Designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)Precinct information board.'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.90.. ** Miyuki-bune (craft) - boat used in the boat ritual of the Sumiyoshi Shrine annual festival. Newly built in Shōwa 8 (1933)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.98.. * Natural monument ** [[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshi shrine sando.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden viewed from approach]][[ファイル:Iki sumiyoshishrine kusu.jpg|サムネイル|"Married camphor trees" in front of haiden]]Two camphor trees of Sumiyoshi Shrine - designated January 1, Shōwa 51 (1976)'Cultural Properties of Iki City,' Iki City Board of Education, Nagasaki, 2017, p.196.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related books === * Edited by Eiji Shirai and Masakuni Toki, 'Shrine Dictionary,' {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|WD=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p.190 === External links === {{commonscat|Sumiyoshi-jinja (Iki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670102.html Sumiyoshi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]]
